title: Unprotected Mint and Burn Functions in vVISR
reason: Rejected: `vVISR` defines its own `owner` storage, initializes it in the constructor, and gates `mint()`/`burn()` with a functioning custom `onlyOwner` modifier.

title: Share Calculation Uses Post-Transfer Balance Without Verification
reason: Rejected: the share calculation happens before either transfer branch executes, not after; the real reportable issue is the unverified contract-path transfer, which is already covered by F-001.

title: Missing Validation for Share Burning in Withdraw
reason: Rejected: `withdraw()` requires `from == msg.sender` or `IVisor(from).owner() == msg.sender`, and `vVISR.burn()` is intentionally callable only by the token owner contract.

title: Inconsistent Compiler Version and Missing License
reason: Rejected: outdated compiler choice and license metadata are not protocol-level vulnerabilities in this codebase.

title: Deposits are priced from the requested amount instead of the actual VISR received
reason: Not kept as a separate finding: the concrete exploitably supported portion is the contract-path under-transfer/no-transfer bug, which is merged into F-001; broader fee-on-transfer or rebasing assumptions about the configured VISR token are too speculative here.
